summary: Small generated decomp-matching PR that attempted to mark ftCo_Attack100.c and grzebes.c as matching and complete three functions: ftCo_800D7268, ftCo_Guard_IASA, and grZebes_801DCCC8. The PR was closed unmerged because the batch worktree commits had build issues that needed to be fixed at the source before batching.
what_changed: Changed configure.py to classify melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c from NonMatching to Matching.
Changed configure.py to classify melee/gr/grzebes.c from NonMatching to Matching.

Replaced the ftCo_800D7268 placeholder in ftCo_Attack100.c with a small callback-style implementation taking void* arg0, treating it as a local struct containing Fighter_GObj* x0 and int x4, calling ftKb_SpecialN_800F1CD8 when x4 == 4, and otherwise returning 0.
Inserted a call to ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) at the start of static inline helper inlineC0 in ftCo_Guard.c, which the PR summary says made ftCo_Guard_IASA match.
Replaced the grZebes_801DCCC8 placeholder in grzebes.c with an implementation that declares f32 slope and intercept, calls Ground_801C4368(&slope, &intercept), and returns grZe_804DB0B0 + slope.
smart_moves: For ftCo_800D7268, the patch used an anonymous local struct cast over a void* callback argument to recover the two observed fields without prematurely introducing a named global type.
For ftCo_Guard_IASA, the matching fix was minimal: adding an existing helper call, ftCo_80092BCC(gobj), inside an inline helper instead of restructuring the larger guard IASA logic.
For grZebes_801DCCC8, the implementation preserved the apparent API behavior of Ground_801C4368 by passing both slope and intercept out-parameters even though only slope is used in the return value.
The configure.py matching status was updated only for files claimed to be fully matching by the PR summary.
decomp_lessons: A tiny missing side-effect call inside an inline helper can be the difference for matching a larger function such as ftCo_Guard_IASA; inspect inlines before rewriting callers.
When a function only uses one value returned through out-parameters, keep the unused out-parameter if the callee signature requires it; deleting it may not match or may misrepresent the API.
For callback/context arguments with unknown layout, a local anonymous struct can be a pragmatic matching tactic, but future cleanup should verify the layout and consider naming the type if it recurs.
Do not rely only on successful local matching percentages in a generated batch; this PR was closed because worktree commits had build issues before batching.
Marking whole objects as Matching in configure.py should be gated by build correctness as well as function-level match claims.
naming_conventions: Unidentified common fighter functions retained address-style names such as ftCo_800D7268, ftCo_800D72A0, and ftCo_800D730C.
Stage-specific Zebes functions use the grZebes_801Dxxxx naming pattern, while the global/static data symbol shown uses grZe_804DB0B0.
Existing unknown struct fields kept x-offset style names such as x221B_b0 and guard movement fields xC and x10.
The callback helper in ftCo_Attack100.c used temporary local field names x0 and x4, matching offset-based decomp convention for unknown layouts.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a local anonymous struct overlay on void* arg0 in ftCo_800D7268 to produce direct x0/x4 field access and a simple branch on x4 == 4.
Returned M2C_UNK from ftCo_800D7268, indicating the exact return type was still not confidently typed in the diff excerpt.
Added ftCo_80092BCC(gobj) before the guard condition in inlineC0, likely to recover an expected side effect/order in the generated assembly for ftCo_Guard_IASA.
Declared two local f32 variables, slope and intercept, and passed both by address to Ground_801C4368 before returning grZe_804DB0B0 + slope.
Updated object matching flags in configure.py from NonMatching to Matching for ftCo_Attack100.c and grzebes.c after the attempted matches.
review_feedback: No inline review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
Issue comment feedback: the PR was closed because worktree commits had build issues that needed to be fixed at the source before batching.
Because the PR was closed unmerged, the matching implementations should be treated as attempted fixes rather than accepted project history.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for accepted matches of ftCo_800D7268, ftCo_Guard_IASA, or grZebes_801DCCC8 after the build issues were fixed.
Search whether the anonymous callback struct used by ftCo_800D7268 appears elsewhere and should be promoted to a named type.
Verify whether ftCo_Attack100.c and grzebes.c were later marked Matching in configure.py by a merged PR.
Investigate the source build issue that caused this generated batch PR to be closed.
